title: Subscriptions panel with drag-and-drop reordering and session management
purpose: Provides a UI component for managing subscribed sessions, allowing users to view, navigate, unsubscribe, and reorder subscriptions with drag-and-drop functionality.
module_context: Exports a SubscriptionsPanel React component that manages subscription state and UI interactions, including drag-and-drop reordering, subscription creation, and session navigation.

steps.content: Calculate the number of seconds elapsed since the last update.
IF less than 60 seconds, return the seconds value with 's' suffix.
ELSE calculate the number of minutes and return with 'm' suffix if less than 60 minutes.
ELSE calculate the number of hours and return with 'h' suffix, including remaining minutes if non-zero.
Initialize a state variable to track the current time.
IF the subscription status is unknown, return null immediately.
Set up an interval to update the current time based on elapsed time.
IF elapsed time is under one minute, update every second.
ELSE update every sixty seconds.
Clear the interval when the component unmounts.
Calculate and return the formatted elapsed time since the last update.

methods.name: SubscriptionRow
methods.normalized_params: ({ subKey, sub, onNavigate, onUnsubscribe, onDragStart, onDragOver, onDragEnd, isDragOver }: { subKey: string; sub: SubscribedSession; onNavigate: (project: string, session: string) => void; onUnsubscribe: (key: string) => void; onDragStart: (e: React.DragEvent, key: string) => void; onDragOver: (e: React.DragEvent, key: string) => void; onDragEnd: () => void; isDragOver: boolean; }): React.ReactElement

steps.content: Calculate the elapsed time since the last update for the subscription.
Determine the background styling based on the subscription status.
Render a row displaying the project and session name.
Display the elapsed time if available.
Include an unsubscribe button that stops event propagation.
Apply drag and drop event handlers to the row.
Apply conditional styling when the row is being dragged over.
Initialize state variables for collapsed status, dropdown visibility, and drag operations.
Use memoization to build an ordered list of subscriptions based on stored order.
Set up drag start handler to store the dragged key.
Set up drag over handler to track the drag over key and prevent default behavior.
Set up drag end handler to reorder subscriptions when drag operation completes.
Build a list of available sessions that are not yet subscribed to.
Set up navigation handler to switch to a selected session.
Set up subscription handler to add a new subscription and close the dropdown.
Store the dragged key in a ref for later use.
Set the drag effect to move.
Prevent the default drag over behavior.
Set the drop effect to move.
Update the drag over key state to track which item is being dragged over.
Retrieve the from and to keys from refs and state.
Reset the drag key ref and drag over key state.
IF either key is null or keys are the same, return early.
Get the ordered keys from the project subscriptions.
Find the indices of the from and to keys in the ordered list.
IF either index is invalid, return early.
Remove the from key from its current position and insert it at the to key's position.
Reorder the subscriptions using the updated key list.
Get all subscription keys from the subscriptions object.
Filter the stored order to only include keys that exist in subscriptions.
Find keys that are in subscriptions but not in the stored order.
Combine the ordered keys with the unordered keys to create a complete ordered list.
Find the target session in the sessions array matching the project and session name.
IF the target session exists, set it as the current session.
Subscribe to the specified project and session.
Close the subscription dropdown.

methods.name: buildAvailableSessions
methods.normalized_params: (): { project: string; name: string; displayName?: string }[]

steps.content: Create a set of keys for currently subscribed sessions.
Filter the sessions to exclude those that are already subscribed.
Sort the remaining sessions by project name and then by session name.
